title: keep_hierarchy Verilog attribute leading to unconstrained top level ports from internal signals
body:
### Version

Yosys 0.61+112 (git sha1 ac427a79b, clang++ 18.1.8 -fPIC -O3)

### On which OS did this happen?

Linux

### Reproduction Steps

I'm not entirely sure, if this is a yosys or nextpnr-himbaechel problem, but using the Verilog attribute "(* keep_hierarchy = "TRUE" *) " somewhere along your module tree leads to generation of a unconstrained port - apparently on the top level module. Deleting "(* keep_hierarchy = "TRUE" *)"  resolves the issue.

```
module TopLevel (
  output wire          uart_txd,
  input  wire          uart_rxd,
  input  wire          clk,
  inout  wire [5:0]    gpioA
);

(...)
UartCtrlRx rx (
    .io_configFrame_dataLength (io_config_frame_dataLength[2:0]), //i
    .io_configFrame_stop       (io_config_frame_stop           ), //i
    .io_configFrame_parity     (io_config_frame_parity[1:0]    ), //i
    .io_samplingTick           (clockDivider_tickReg           ), //i
    .io_read_valid             (rx_io_read_valid               ), //o
    .io_read_ready             (io_read_ready                  ), //i
    .io_read_payload           (rx_io_read_payload[7:0]        ), //o
    .io_rxd                    (io_uart_rxd                    ), //i
    .io_rts                    (rx_io_rts                      ), //o
    .io_error                  (rx_io_error                    ), //o
    .io_break                  (rx_io_break                    ), //o
    .clk                       (clk                            ), //i
    .axiReset                  (axiReset                       )  //i
  );
(...)

endmodule

module UartCtrlRx (
  input  wire [2:0]    io_configFrame_dataLength,
  input  wire [0:0]    io_configFrame_stop,
  input  wire [1:0]    io_configFrame_parity,
  input  wire          io_samplingTick,
  output wire          io_read_valid,
  input  wire          io_read_ready,
  output wire [7:0]    io_read_payload,
  input  wire          io_rxd,
  output wire          io_rts,
  output reg           io_error,
  output wire          io_break,
  input  wire          clk,
  input  wire          axiReset
);

(...)
 (* keep_hierarchy = "TRUE" *) BufferCC_1 io_rxd_buffercc (
    .io_dataIn  (io_rxd                    ), //i
    .io_dataOut (io_rxd_buffercc_io_dataOut), //o
    .clk        (clk                       ), //i
    .axiReset   (axiReset                  )  //i
  );
(...)

endmodule
```

Generation script:
```
yosys  -DSYNTHESIS -l yosys.log -p "read_verilog -DSYNTHESIS -sv ../hw/gen/*.v; synth_gowin -family gw2a -top TopLevel -json build.yosys.json"
nextpnr-himbaechel --parallel-refine --log nextpnr.log --placer-heap-timingweight 100 --placer-heap-cell-placement-timeout 0 --detailed-timing-report --device GW2AR-LV18QN88C8/I7 --vopt family=GW2A-18C --vopt cst=../constraints/tangnano20k.cst --freq 27 --timing-allow-fail --seed 1 --json build.yosys.json --write build.nextpnr.json
(...)
```




### Expected Behavior

No toplevel port from internal signals.

### Actual Behavior

nextpnr-himbaechel --version:
`"nextpnr-himbaechel" -- Next Generation Place and Route (Version nextpnr-0.9-59-g8c627817)
`


Nextpnr output with keep_hierarchy enabled:

code_blocks:
module TopLevel (
  output wire          uart_txd,
  input  wire          uart_rxd,

(...)

    .io_read_ready             (io_read_ready                  ), //i

(...)

yosys  -DSYNTHESIS -l yosys.log -p "read_verilog -DSYNTHESIS -sv ../hw/gen/*.v; synth_gowin -family gw2a -top TopLevel -json build.yosys.json"
